blocks.content:
## Trial readiness

Use this checklist on any Grafana Cloud stack before you run the spike—especially during a trial or evaluation when stakeholders are watching. Grafana Cloud k6 and observability must already reach the workload you test. If a signal in the table is missing, close that gap before you run the spike—or record the gap in your scratch pad when the path tells you how.

| If this is missing | Start here |
| --- | --- |
| No completed Grafana Cloud k6 run yet | [Run your first k6 performance test](/docs/learning-paths/run-first-k6-test/) |
| No traces in Tempo for the service under test | [Send traces to Grafana Cloud using Alloy](/docs/learning-paths/send-traces-alloy/) |
| No **View Traces** on the k6 test result | [Correlate results with Grafana Cloud Traces](/docs/grafana-cloud/testing/k6/analyze-results/integration-with-grafana-cloud-traces/) |
| No application logs in Loki | [Send logs to Grafana Cloud using Alloy](/docs/learning-paths/send-logs-alloy-loki/) |
| No synthetic probe metrics in **Explore** | [Detect customer-visible outages using Synthetic Monitoring](/docs/learning-paths/detect-outages-synthetic-monitoring/) |
| Stakeholders expect a saved dashboard, not only **Explore** | [Correlate results in Grafana](/docs/grafana-cloud/testing/k6/analyze-results/correlate-results-in-grafana/) and the optional dashboard steps in **Prepare a live metrics view** |

You are a performance engineer spiking quickpizza before launch. The test met every threshold; your job is to decide whether the system is actually ready by chaining probe metrics, application logs, and traces with the same k6 run in one UTC window.

**Traces:** Use the k6 **HTTP** tab and **View Traces** when your instrumented system sends spans to your Tempo tenant. This lab also uses **Explore** for metrics and logs. A passing spike against public QuickPizza does not by itself put server traces in your Tempo data source.

Record the k6 test start time in UTC and reuse that range for metrics and logs in **Explore**. Finish the spike and **Explore** milestones in one sitting when you can so retention does not drop telemetry.

Copy the scratch pad into your notes and fill the **Your value** column as you go. You reuse these fields in [Postmortem narrative](/docs/learning-paths/correlate-k6-observability/synthesize-findings/).

| Signal | What to capture | Your value |
| --- | --- | --- |
| k6 | Test start time (UTC) | |
| k6 | p95 `http_req_duration` (ms) | |
| k6 | Peak VU minute (UTC or offset from start) | |
| Probe metrics | Highest-latency probe region + peak latency (ms) | |
| Logs | Representative error message text or parsed field | |
| Traces | k6 **View Traces** summary **or** documented readiness gap | |

Check your work: you can move on when the scratch pad is copied and you know your UTC anchor time. If a data source fails in the next milestone, open the troubleshooting topics for this step.

In the next milestone you align `grafanacloud-k6` and application metrics on one **Explore** time range.
